description_long: This is a rectangular wooden cowshed with a prominent gable roof clad in reddish-brown shingles. The front features large double barn doors with visible black metal hinges and a partially open entryway, suggesting easy access for livestock or equipment. The side and rear walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving the structure a rustic, utilitarian appearance. The roof extends slightly beyond the walls, providing some overhang for weather protection. There are no visible windows, but small square ventilation openings are present near the roofline on the gable ends. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of agricultural outbuildings found in rural or peri-urban settings.
description: A wooden cowshed with a red gable roof, large double barn doors, and small ventilation openings near the roofline. The structure is rustic and functional, with no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing large double barn doors, a partially open entry, and the overhanging red gable roof.
description_view_1: Side gable end view displaying horizontal wooden planks and two small square ventilation openings near the roof peak.
description_view_2: Top-down view highlighting the full expanse of the red gable roof and the rectangular footprint of the building.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end view, similar to view 1, with horizontal wooden planks and two small ventilation openings near the roof peak.
attribute_door_style: double wooden sliding barn doors with black metal hinges
attribute_window_presence: no windows; only small square ventilation openings near roofline
attribute_ventilation_features: small square ventilation openings near gable peaks
